bugs_found: CRITICAL: POST /v1/auth/siwe/nonce deterministically returns HTTP 500 internal_error for every valid input tested (given address 0x742d35Cc..., lowercase variant, valid-EIP-55 variant 0x742D35cC..., 0xd8dA6BF... vitalik.eth checksummed, and 0x000...000 zero-address). Error detail: 'invalid message: max line number was 6 / Invalid message: {success:false,state:103,length:369,matched:0,maxMatched:167,...}'. The server generates a SIWE message then its own EIP-4361 parser rejects it (maxMatched 167 of 369 chars, state 103). This blocks all SIWE login.

Sub-bug: parser rejects checksum form selected by server -- 0x742d35Cc6634C0532925a3b844Bc9e7595f8beA0 (given input) triggers 'line 2: invalid EIP-55 address - 0x742d35Cc...', suggesting the server echoes the raw address without normalising to a valid EIP-55 checksum before building the message.

Sub-bug: even with the trivially valid zero address, the failure occurs without the EIP-55 prefix (just 'invalid message: max line number was 6'). Message generation itself is malformed beyond checksum (e.g., missing/blank field on lines 3-6, since only 167 of 369 chars matched).
Response leaks raw ABNF parser internals (state/maxMatched/nodeHits/maxTreeDepth) in detail to clients -- should be logged server-side, not returned in a public error.
Could not verify requirements #2 (EIP-4361 format) or #3 (chain_id == 133) because nonce endpoint never returns a 200 body.
notes: Negative-path behaviour on both endpoints is correct Problem+JSON: missing address -> 400 validation_error 'address: Required'; malformed address -> 400 'address must be 0x + 40 hex chars'; empty verify body -> 400 'message: Required; signature: Required'; invalid verify message+signature -> 401 invalid_credentials with agent_action 'Token signature invalid or malformed. Re-run SIWE.' OPTIONS returns 204 with permissive CORS and full security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, etc.). GET returns 405. The happy path (#1-3) is entirely blocked by the 500 bug; request_id values surfaced for support lookup: req_01KPV3T4Q9..., req_01KPV3TTDSMQ..., req_01KPV3V5NDKX...
